title: Imperial Sentinel
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Sentinel}} {{Organization |image=[[File:Imperial_sentinels_EGF.jpg]] |name=Imperial Sentinels |type= |founder=[[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]''[[The Force Unleashed Campaign Guide]]'' |leader=*[[Sedriss QL]]''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' *[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]][[Dark Empire 4|''Dark Empire'' 4]] *[[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adepts]]''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' |subgroup= |headquarters=[[Emperor's Citadel]], [[Byss/Legends|Byss]] |locations= |formed= |founded=Before [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]The ''Dark Empire Sourcebook'' indicates that the Imperial Sentinels were known to have been active at the time of [[Ashka Boda]]'s capture which, according to [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']], occurred in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. |fragmented= |reorganized= |dissolved= |restored= |affiliation=[[Dark Empire]] }} {{Quote|Who are these big, ugly guys in the armor? They look familiar.''"
"''These are Imperial Sentinels, the elite warriors of the Empire.|Luke Skywalker and an Imperial Dungeoneer|audio=LukeDungeoneer.flac|[[Dark Empire (audio drama)|''Dark Empire'' audio drama]]}} '''Imperial Sentinels''' were armored-and-cloaked warriors assigned to serve [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adepts]]. [[Cloning/Legends|Cloned]] in hidden facilities on the secret [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] throne [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Byss/Legends|Byss]], these mute giants were [[Sith alchemy/Legends|mutated]] by Dark Side Adepts and injected with chemicals that suppressed their higher brain functions, causing them to become completely subservient. For the duration of their natural existence, Sentinels were slaves to their assigned Adept and carried out their master's bidding without question. They accompanied the Adepts on missions off-world, typically acting as bodyguards. Mainly remaining on Byss to guard the [[Emperor's Citadel]], Sentinels had only been seen a handful of times, leading to a great deal of speculation among the upper echelons of the Dark Empire as to what exactly these armored behemoths were. In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], after defecting, was known to have bred at least two of his own Sentinels. After a bold attempt to rescue Skywalker from Byss, [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]]—Skywalker's sister and prominent [[New Republic Provisional Council|New Republic Council]] member—and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]—Leia's husband and [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] of the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]—were escorted by Skywalker's Sentinels to Palpatine's clone laboratories on Byss. There they confronted both Skywalker and Palpatine himself. [[11 ABY/Legends|One year later]], most of the Sentinels and their cloning facilities were obliterated with the [[destruction of Byss]] and the final death of Palpatine. ==Organization and origins== {{Quote|Luke's brought more of those Sentinel goons with him. You must rate pretty high to get the Emperor's personal bodyguards.|Han Solo|audio=HanSentinels.flac|}} [[File:ImperialSentinel-DE2.jpg|thumb|left|150px|An Imperial Sentinel on Byss]] The origins of the Imperial Sentinels remained a mystery for most in even the highest levels of the former [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Even the [[Imperial Sovereign Protector]]s, an elite cadre of [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guardsmen]] trained in the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], had no idea, nor interest, in how the Sentinels came to be. The Sentinels were in actuality an early experiment of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and his inner circle of dark-side [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-users]] to create a race of beings completely subservient to their will. Sentinels were [[Cloning/Legends|cloned]] from tissue stock in hidden facilities on the Imperial fortress [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Byss/Legends|Byss]], where chemicals were introduced into their bodies during the gestation period, suppressing higher mental abilities such as speech. This tissue sample likely originated from [[Zeta Magnus (clone)|the clone]] of Darth Sidious' traitorous generalissimo, [[Zeta Magnus]], who was [[Sai tok/Legends|bisected]] at the [[Battle of Skye]] and spirited away by [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]]. As the real Zeta Magnus observed galactic events from his [[Dark Worlds|kingdom]] in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], Darth Sidious created clones of him, dressed them up in his armor, and suppressed their mental capacity as a mockery of his intelligence.{{JCFCite|url=posts/52222960|board=Literature|thread=Abel G. Peña's "Skyewalkers" and "Lone Wolf"|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|quote=Part of the way I developed Zeta Magnus was by interpolating from the Imperial Sentinels on Byss. The Dark Empire Sourcebook suggested it was a mystery whether they were clones or cyborgs, and this inspired my idea that the ATHAs were a kind of cloned army created by the Arkanians. It was also the reason I slipped just a bit of the Amorphiian DNA (or whatever passes for DNA with those guys) into Magnus' make up. The Imperial Sentinels were also described as "mute giants" whose brain functions were intentionally suppressed. This suggested to me that Magnus should be the categorical opposite of this: verbose and very intelligent. I figured Palpatine would've created the Imperial Sentinels as a mockery of Magnus.|archiveurl=https://boards.theforce.net/threads/abel-g-pe%C3%B1as-skyewalkers-and-lone-wolf.50027998/page-5#post-52222960}} During the growth cycle, Sentinels were mutated by [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adepts]] through [[Sith alchemy/Legends|Sith alchemy]] as part of their training, during which the Adepts would attempt to form a mental bond with the Sentinels. If the process was successful, the Sentinels would end up as mindless living hulks totally dependent upon the will of the Adepts for purpose or movement. If the process was unsuccessful, the would-be sentries were simply disposed of. First-generation Imperial Sentinels were deemed too dangerous to keep around, leading to rumors that they may have been cloned from Force-sensitive individuals. Later generations were much less powerful, but far easier to control.{{DoD|Jaws of the Sarlacc}} Sentinels functioned primarily as bodyguards for Dark Side Adepts. Whenever Palpatine sent an Adept or [[Executor (Imperial rank)|Executor]] [[Sedriss QL|Sedriss]] on a high-priority mission for the Empire, one or more of these titans would escort them off-world. Few of these creatures existed, however, and when not on-duty with an Adept, they would remain on Byss to protect the [[Emperor's Citadel]].''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' Some Sentinels also guarded the more secure and private areas of the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].''[[Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook]]'' ==History== {{Quote|My master wishes to speak with you. These Sentinels will escort you to the Emperor's presence.''"
"''The ''Emperor''?''"
"''Yes, he lives.|Luke Skywalker and Leia Organa Solo|audio=LukeLeiaSentinels.flac|}} The Imperial Sentinels made their first known appearance at the scene of a battle in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] on an unnamed planet during the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]] where several [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] had been captured by Imperial forces. The Sentinels carried authentic [[Datachip/Legends|datachips]] allowing them to take custody of a single prisoner: the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Ashka Boda]]. Boda, a member of the [[Librarian's Assembly]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]],{{DB|character|yaddle|Yaddle}} held in his possession the [[Tedryn Holocron]], an ancient [[Jedi artifact/Legends|Jedi artifact]] containing the knowledge and teachings of Jedi Masters long past. The Sentinels escorted Boda from the battle back to their [[Lictor-class dungeon ship|dungeon ship]] and flew him to Coruscant, where he was tortured and executed by Emperor Palpatine. Palpatine took possession of the Tedryn Holocron and used its vast repository of information to help create ''[[The Book of Anger]]'', the first volume in his ''[[Dark Side Compendium]]''.[[Dark Empire endnotes|''Dark Empire'' endnotes]], part 4: ''For a Thousand Generations'' In that same year, first-generation Sentinels were present on a [[Golan space defense platform]] guarding a [[Super Star Destroyer prototype]] orbiting Byss as part of the Empire's [[Sarlacc Project]]. A group of [[Alderaanian Resistance]] agents boarded the [[Space station/Legends|space station]] during the [[Battle of Byss]] and confronted these Sentinels in [[Hangar/Legends|Docking Bay 616]]. [[File:Leia_sentinels.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Luke Skywalker and his Sentinels confront Leia.]] As of [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], the Sentinels were still in their early stages of development and rarely left Byss. Occasionally, however, Sentinels would be seen briefly in public taking custody of Force-users—often Jedi—while in transit to Imperial Center or other locations. This led to a large amount of speculation and gossip among the Imperial court about the Sentinels and where they came from. Some believed that the Sentinels originated from a captured [[Subspecies/Legends|race]] of giants while others believed them to be [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborgs]] or [[Droid/Legends|droids]]. Some even speculated that they were formed from a [[Species/Legends|species]] who once openly rebelled against the Emperor and paid the ultimate price for their disobedience. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|Alliance Special Forces]] heard unconfirmed rumors of the Sentinels. They were rumored to be a special unit consisting of giant, armored warriors who guarded the Emperor as well as his most favored servants and [[Imperial Advisor/Legends|advisors]].''[[Rules of Engagement: The Rebel SpecForce Handbook]]'' The Sentinels would not see widespread use until over a [[Standard decade/Legends|decade]] later in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]] after the launch of [[Operation Shadow Hand]], the "reborn" Palpatine's military campaigns against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and its allies to once again take control of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Operating out of Byss, Palpatine made use of [[Imperial Dungeoneer]]s and dungeon ships to ferry and administrate prisoners of war. Sentinels often worked in tandem with Dungeoneers, escorting prisoners such as [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and hero of the New Republic, to facilities on Byss. After Skywalker's defection to the Empire, he apprenticed himself to the resurrected Palpatine and bred two of his own Sentinels. [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], New Republic [[New Republic Provisional Council|councilor]] and Skywalker's sister, her husband [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and former New Republic [[General/Legends|general]], and the droid [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] later made an attempt to rescue Skywalker from Byss and flew the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] straight into the Emperor's Citadel. Skywalker appeared before Leia and had his two Sentinels escort the ''Falcon''{{'s}} crew to Palpatine's clone laboratories hidden beneath the surface of Byss. After realizing how far her brother had fallen to the dark side and finding out that Palpatine was still alive, Leia attacked and severed the right hand one of these Sentinels when it restrained Han under Palpatine's orders. This resulted in Leia's capture and the imprisonment of the rest of the ''Falcon''{{'s}} crew. As Operation Shadow Hand progressed, Sentinels continued to see use as guards in the Emperor's Citadel even after the [[Battle of Balmorra]].[[Dark Empire II 1|''Dark Empire II'' 1]] In [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], Palpatine's newest flagship, ''[[Eclipse II]]'', was boarded by a New Republic strike team led by [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] and [[Kam Solusar]]. The [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] sabotaged ''Eclipse II''{{'s}} [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] system and sent the [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]] on a collision course with the planet Byss. ''Eclipse II'' exited [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] too close to the [[Galaxy Gun]], the Emperor's latest [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]], and caused the cannon to fire prematurely. The projectile collided with Byss, [[Destruction of Byss|destroying]] the planet along with many of the Sentinels stationed there and their cloning facilities.[[Empire's End 2|''Empire's End'' 2]] ==Characteristics== {{Quote|Hey, you overgrown freak! ''Let go of my husband''!|Leia Organa Solo attacks a Sentinel|audio=LeiaSentinel.flac|}} [[File:PalpyAndSentinel-EGPM.jpg|thumb|left|150px|The clone Palpatine beside an Imperial Sentinel]] Gargantuan in size, Imperial Sentinels were easily distinguishable from any of Palpatine's other minions; said to be twice the size and strength of other Imperial guards.{{KennerCite|set=Star Wars: The Power of the Force (1995 toy line)|pack=Imperial Sentinel|link=POTF2EUimperialsentinel.asp}} The sight alone of a Sentinel following in the wake of a Dark Side Adept was enough to strike fear into the hearts of the Emperor's enemies. Sentinels were mute due to the introduction of harmful chemicals into their bodies which also caused them to lose the ability to think for themselves. They were [[Mutant|altered]] through the use of Sith alchemy and completely dependent upon the will of Adepts, only able to do what they were told. It was rumored that Adepts had the ability to view what Sentinels perceived, thus allowing them to maintain complete control of the titans; however this claim was never verified. The primary choice of weapons for Sentinels were large [[Vibro-ax/Legends|vibro-axes]], though they were also proficient in the use of [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]]. They also had training in both martial arts and beast-riding. Palpatine adorned the Sentinels in a uniform similar to the one worn by [[Magus Sentinel|Onderon's Magi Sentinels]] and the mad [[Geneticist/Legends|geneticist]] Zeta Magnus.''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]'' Sentinels donned heavy [[battle armor]] under long, reddish-purple cloaks emblazoned with intricate gold patterns at the chest, back, and shoulders. They wore large helmets that concealed both the head and neck, leaving only their glowing red eyes visible. No parts of their bodies were left uncovered. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:GuardPrimeSentinel.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Left to right: Emperor's Royal Guard concept art, Atha Prime, an Imperial Sentinel]] The Imperial Sentinels made their first appearance in the [[Dark Empire 2|second issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series in [[1992]] as background characters[[Dark Empire 2|''Dark Empire'' 2]] and later in the series's [[Dark Empire 4|fourth issue]] where they played a larger role in the story. They returned [[1994|two years later]] for a brief appearance in [[Dark Empire II 1|''Dark Empire II'' 1]], the first issue of the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire II]]'' comic series. Their background was greatly expounded upon in the ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' in [[1993]] along with several other ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[roleplaying game]] (RPG) supplements released over the years. The Sentinels also made a brief appearance in the first of the [[Dark Empire (audio drama)|''Dark Empire'' audio adaptations]] in 1994, which was notable for being the only source to give the Sentinels voices. In [[2009]] the Sentinels made their final [[in-universe]] appearance in ''[[Jaws of the Sarlacc]]'', the final adventure in Wizards of the Coast's ''[[Dawn of Defiance]]'' campaign in the [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]. The original design for the Imperial Sentinels could be traced back to [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']] concept designs for the [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Emperor's Royal Guard]] by [[Nilo Rodis-Jamero]].{{SWArchive|url=episode-i/bts/design/f20000313/index.html|text=Shades of Blue and Red}}{{DB|organization|emperorsroyalguard|Emperor's Royal Guard}} One design depicted a pair of sentries wielding battle axes similar to those used by the Sentinels in ''Dark Empire''. This sketch was later used to depict a group of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] known as the [[Taloron Hunters]] in ''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]''.''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]'' Another design, more closely resembling the Sentinels, was used to create the character [[Atha Prime/Legends|Atha Prime]], a [[Clone Master/Legends|Clone Master]] who was to be the antagonist for a new storyline set after ''Return of the Jedi''. Titled ''[[The Epic Continues]]'', this storyline was to be adapted into a new toy line in [[1986]] by the toy company [[Kenner Products|Kenner]]. Several design sketches were drawn, including one of Prime, who strongly resembled a Royal Guardsman from the ''Return of the Jedi'' concept art. However, [[Lucasfilm]] rejected the proposal for the new storyline, and the toy line was effectively canceled. In [[1998]], an Imperial Sentinel action figure was released as part of Kenner's ''Expanded Universe'' series, a subset of the larger [[Star Wars: The Power of the Force (1995 toy line)|''Star Wars: The Power of the Force'']] line. In 2009, an Imperial Sentinel miniature was released as part of the [[Jedi Academy (Star Wars Miniatures)|''Jedi Academy'']] expansion to [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars Miniatures]]'' tabletop game.''[[Star Wars Miniatures]]'': [[Jedi Academy (Star Wars Miniatures)|''Jedi Academy'']] {{C|Miniature: [http://www.wizards.com/swminis/swminis_autocard.asp?id=imperia_sentinel&set=JA Imperial Sentinel]}} [[File:SentinelFigures.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Kenner's action figure (left) and Wizards of the Coast's miniature (right)]] In [[2008]] the PlayStation 2, PlayStation Portable and Nintendo Wii versions of the [[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' video game]] introduced a new subdivision of the Royal Guard known only as the "Imperial Sentinels." These Sentinels guarded the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and bore no resemblance to the Imperial Sentinels introduced in ''Dark Empire''.{{TFU|version=PS2, PSP, and Wii}} 2009's ''[[Galaxy at War]]'' RPG supplement by Wizards of the Coast established these Sentinels as the [[Imperial Senate Sentinel]]s, a subdivision of the [[Imperial Senate Guard/Legends|Imperial Senate Guard]].''[[Galaxy at War]]'' ===''Dark Empire'' audio drama voice inconsistencies=== {{Quote|Prisoner secured.|One of the Sentinels' distinctive phrases in the ''Dark Empire'' audio drama|audio=Sentinel-PrisonersSecured.flac|}} The ''Dark Empire'' audio adaptation was released in 1994, three years after the release of the original comic series. The audio drama was notable for including extra scenes not found in the comics as well as changing much of the comic's dialogue. This created numerous continuity errors between the audio drama and comic series. For the purposes of this article, the comics as well as most other sources take precedence over the audio drama. One noticeable continuity error concerning Sentinel speech could be heard throughout the audio drama where several Sentinels were given the ability to speak despite the fact that numerous sources, including the ''Dark Empire Sourcebook'', specifically stated that Sentinels did not possess this ability. In the audio drama, the Sentinels spoke in loud, booming voices and were typically restrained to simple phrases such as "halt, droid" and "prisoner secured," with rare instances where they could be heard muttering near-complete sentences. These lines did not appear in the ''Dark Empire'' comic series and were exclusive to the audio drama. ==Appearances== *{{DoD|Jaws of the Sarlacc}} *{{GalaxiesNGE}} {{Mo}} *[[Dark Empire (audio
